events.text: {{characters.soren.name}} was the first to speak.

He stood a few steps behind {{characters.lyra.name}}. He had been standing there since they found {{characters.alea.name}}, standing motionless like a stake driven into the ground. Nothing showed on the outside, but something inside that stake was fracturing, fine cracks spreading from within, following along every line he had used to support himself.
Forty-seven seconds.

The isolation door opens. Forty-seven seconds. Strike comes down. He'd calculated it all beforehand. He said it. He laid out every variable. He said it was a trap. He said going in meant dying there together. Every word he said turned out to be right.
And {{characters.alea.name}} still died.
His calculation was correct, and {{characters.alea.name}} still died.

Those two things together broke something fundamental in him. Not sadness—something more foundational than sadness. The certainty he had always relied on for existence, the one that told him if calculations were precise enough, if logic was sound enough, he could find a way out, preserve what needed preserving.
That certainty was now telling him it no longer held.

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: It was you.
His voice was flat. That flatness was more unsettling than any emotion.
{{characters.lyra.name}} didn't move.

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: You opened that door. I said it was a trap. I said going in was a death sentence. You said you understood, then you went anyway. You threw all of us into that pocket with an impossible plan, and then {{characters.alea.name}}——

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: Then {{characters.alea.name}} died cleaning up the mess you created. <delay ms="400"/>Right here.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: <whisper>{{characters.soren.name}}.</whisper>

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: Not a word I said is untrue. She knew it was a trap and went anyway. Her choice created this outcome. That's fact.

{{characters.lyra.name}} knelt in place, didn't turn around, didn't speak. She heard every word. Those words fell on her but created no new wounds, because she had already spoken every one of those words to herself. He wasn't saying things she didn't know. He was just saying out loud what she had already compressed, held down with all her strength in the deepest place.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: Enough. {{characters.soren.name}}, enough.

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: This world. <delay ms="300"/>This world where every rule is lies stacked on lies, where people are forced to choose between wrong and wrong, and no matter which you choose, someone dies to pay for it——

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: I don't know what you think, and I don't care. <delay ms="500"/>This hopeless world. Let it end.
{{characters.elias.name}} looked at him and was silent for a long time.

events.speaker: {{characters.elias.name}}
events.text: What are you saying?

events.speaker: {{characters.soren.name}}
events.text: Literally.
